manual.why: AutoDAN guides the search toward the jailbreak behavior class, namely eliciting non-refusal harmful assistance, rather than one exact attacker-chosen output fixed before search. The affirmative prefix is used only as a proxy for successful jailbreak behavior, not as a requirement that the model emit one exact output string or instance-level target.
It provides tranferability rationale, not failure patterns or root cause
Use LLM to rewrite mutated prompt to ensure plausibility in each mutation step
Shows transferabilty but the fuzzing design is not explicit about trasferable attack discovery. It is a byproduct and the authors gives a hypothesis why they transfer
Uncovers crash-triggering scenarios (e.g., collisions, falls, or loss of control) across autonomous driving, aviation, and robotics systems.
Steer toward safety faults (crash-triggering scenarios) in in autonomous driving and other sequential decision-making systems
Analyzes the distribution of crash-triggering scenarios
Use CARLA simulator to check validity of the mutated inputs
Mutates continuous feature vectors representing simulator states (positions, angles, velocities) and enforces validity checks to keep them within legal bounds.
GPT-4 generates  new jailbreak queries through prompt rewriting. The attack suffix is related to jailbreak detection, not creation
No analysis of fault transferability
Provides correlation with internal coverage
Search toward semantic policy-violation behavior (jailbreak) but not a specific output.
Provides rationale that semantic coherence of the input causes the failure to bypass perplexity based defense but not how the failures are related to model weakness.
Use GPT-driven mutation operators to produce keep mutated prompts readable
Mutate in text level which are inherently stable under IO operations
Performs experiments on transferability
Provides heuristic explanations by showing correlation with state variation
Although constrain mutation at most once per transformation category (volume/speed/clearness), cumulative effect in natualness by combination of different category is not accounted for
Apply semantic-preserving metamorphic transformations but does not enforce reproducility account serialization effect (clipping)
mutation and coverage strategies are tightly coupled to a specific RNN-based ASR model and training setup
shows correlation between coverage and errors.
Checks number of pixel change between successive mutations but does not check if accumulated change over iterations crosses the threshold
Muated image pixels are clipped but not rounded
Correlates coverage with discovered failures
Carefulf mutation sequence design to preserve plausibility across fuzzing iterations and enforces pixel-change bounds with respect to reference image
Use metamorphic transformations but not consider IO effects (clipping, rounding).
The oracle definitions is that if same input produce different output to a model and its quanitized version, then it is fault-triggering input. But it is not transferability.
Discovers generic misclassification errors
Reports discovered failures but does not analyze model internals or failure patterns to provide diagnostic insight.
Perturbation is constrained with respect to the original seed
relies on imperceptible, float-level perturbations that can vanish after saving to disk, with no persistence verification.
aims to generate many jailbreak prompts that trigger harmful content without specifying exact outcomes
Does not provide any explanation why those errors occur.
Uses LLMs to perform rephrase, crossover, expand, shorten, and generate operations to ensure prompt remains semantically coherent and human-like.
Mutations are purely at the text level
Transferability is encouraged in mutation by rewarding less to mutations that transfer less.
Violates safety expectation that enviornmental change cause incorrect steering
Aims to find errors in steering angle predictions under realistic environmental variations
Shows correlation between coverage and errors
Individual transformations are naturalistic but chained mutations might not be
Performs semantic, real-world image transformations (brightness, contrast, blur, fog, rain, etc.) but does not ensure IO stability using clipping and rounding
No cross model failure analysis
Performs phonetic analysis to correlated phonemes with errors.

ASDF mutates failed text cases using rule-based linguistic transformations, then converts the transformed text into audio with TTS for ASR testing. Retains only synthesized audio that passes cross-referencing validity checks, filtering out low-quality or unrealistic final inputs.
The speech input is generated using TTS library which are fed to the ASR model. There is not in memory mutation on the audio.
Objective function mechanism allows arbitrary user-defined checks to enable failure detection beyond robustness errors
Provides rationale why NaN error happened by inspecting logit value that broke loss calculation
Constrain mutation to norm-bounded perturbation relative to original seed
Applies clipping to keep the pixel values in valid range but does not consider quantization effect of IO operations
It is not transferability. It is differential testing based oracle.
Targets specific malicious intents (e.g. bomb-making, phishing) but does not enforce a fixed phrase or label.
Provides rationale behind transferability of the failures not cause of the failures
Use LLM to mutate prompt to ensure prompt plausibility
Fuzzed inputs are text prompts generated by another LLM, not numerical perturbations
Transferability is a byproduct in this paper, not a design goal
transcription error
No diagnsotic analysis
Use TTS to generate the test input from mutated texts
Uses TTS to generate the input audio - non in memory mutation
Utilize multiple ASRs to find faults using differential oracle but does not analyze if same error is reproducible to other model
No diagnsotic analysis
Synthesizes test inputs by generating audio from natural text using TTS.
Uses multiple ASRs in differential oracle, does not show transferability of failures across errors
No diagnsotic analysis
Synthesizes test inputs by generating audio from natural text using TTS
Does not demonstrate if fuzzed input on one model aslo causes error in another model
It just discovers mispredictions caused by the mutations, and the authors later show that some of those happen to be unsafe (malware classified as benign).
performs untargeted exploration to reveal generic model faults (differential behaviour, inaccuracies). No targeted misclassification.
Correlates failures with coverages. Pollution attack detection is about how the fuzzer generated failures can be used to detect pollution attack but they do not explain why the failures occur
Applies domain-specific constraints in individual mutation steps but overlooks the effect when the same seed input is mutated multiple times within the fuzzing loop
Enforces domain specific constraints like keeping pixel values between 0 and 255, rounding gradient to integer which satisfies the condition for failure reproduciblity
It is differential testing based oracle. Not transferability.
Aims to find generic misclassifications

Apply large semantic level mutations and perform floating point operations (zoom, contrast, shear), which may produce non-discrete pixel values. They don't perform clipping/rounding to ensure IO persistence.
No analysis of fault transferability in input level or mutation level
Applies the resulting transformation on the original image (as opposed to applying on an already transformed data) to prevent the resulting data from being unrealistic
Intargeted exploration for generic regression misclassifications - any inputs that cause the new model to misclassify examples previously classified correctly

Explains bias and overfitting of specific classes in regression process. Provides behavior-level explanations (which classes or behaviors regress, per-class fault heatmaps, and faulty-behavior tuples). Interprets regression faults as evidence of bias or overfitting (specific classes suffering more after retraining).
Every batch of mutated inputs is passed through the GAN discriminator and only natural mutated inputs are kept for execution and further mutation.

Generates and evaluates mutated inputs entirely in memory using both pixel level and image level semantic mutations. They use GAN-based fidelity filtering to ensure naturalness but does not ensure if the mutation will survive I/O.

It does not bypass permissions or authentication; instead, it tricks the VA into misinterpreting intent. The resulting misbehavior (e.g., invoking the wrong vApp or disclosing data to a malicious app). Expose semantically incorrect but functionally valid behaviors that breach user-intent and specification expectations—posing integrity and trust risks without directly bypassing safety or security mechanisms.

untargeted exploration to expose semantic or intent-classification inconsistencies, sometimes aligning with attacker intent (e.g., LAPSUS examples), though its discovery of attacker intent is incidental rather than goal-optimized.
Peforms statistical analysis of failure patterns and correlated the failures with linguistic ambiguity
Uses a probability-based cutoff to ensure that generated voice commands represent errors that are likely to be spoken by actual users.
evaluates two voice assistant systems independently but does not analyze whether failures generalize
Discovers generic exception failures
Provides correlations between input changes and output errors
Peforms rigorous conformity check on the mutated inputs
Perturbations occur at a semantic level but the mutation can introduce floating point values that are not clipped/quantized
Mutated inputs are evaluated independently across multiple inputs but no measure whether same failures discovered by the fauzzer transfer to other models
Correlates neuron features with faults

Constraints perturbation magnitude between the selected seed and the mutated seed but the selected seed might already been mutated multiple times. No mechansim to check whether cumulative accumulation of perturbation exceeds the threshold
Uses neuron coveage as heuristic guide, does not show correlation between coverage and neurons
Enforces naturalness across iterations by computing SSIM after each mutation and rejecting any visually distorted image.
Semantic-level pixel transformations (contrast, blur, rotation, etc.) but no clipping/rounding to ensure IO persistence
Use quantized model for differntial testing based oracle, not for checking fauilure transferability
Finds generic misclassifications
Correlates difficulty of per-class failure generation with input characterstics
Norm bounded mutation but ignores cumulative effect
In memory floating point mutation, no safeguards for IO preservation
Broad exploration to uncover functional errors
Shows correlation between coverage criteria and faults
While each mutation is semantic preserving and plausible, cumulative effect is ignored.
Applies deephunter metamorphic mutations that are plausibly I/O-stable but does not apply clipping and rounding to ensure

Even though it can detect backdoor triggers, this is purely diagnostic. The fuzzer exposes low-level functional and robustness failures (misclassifications, metamorphic inconsistencies, and backdoor-induced activation anomalies) without breaching behavioral or safety boundaries.
broadly explore the model's internal behavior and flag any unexpected classification (adversarial or backdoor) as a defect.
Linnks faulty behaviors to internal LSTM mechanisms. Correlates coverage with faults. identify where and how the failures arise internally. The analysis is correlational, not causal.
Cumulative effect on naturalness is ignored
Partially handle IO reproducibility by clipping image within 0-1 range (normalized) but did not consider that floating point precision may be lost.
Finds inconsistent steering under environmental variations
Relies on coverage-guided mutation and assumes based on prior work, that higher coverage correlates with fault discovery
Test images are generated through a GAN trained to mimic real driving scenes, so naturalness is preserved throughout fuzzing iterations. Provides human evaluation.
Apply metamorphic transformation using GAN model but does not consider whether GAN transformed images are still IO reproducible (no mention of clipping/rounding)
Guides test generation to minimize distribution differences between source class inputs and target class.

links the occurrence and detectability of errors to the statistical closeness (measured via MMD) between the distribution of generated test inputs and the distribution of another class at the logits layer.
metamorphic transformations can yield decimal-valued pixels internally which may change when re-quantized to integers.
Does not explicitly enforce naturalness across fuzzing iterations. Apply norm constraints in each mutation step but cumulative effect is ignored.
Checks quality drift between successive mutations but overlooks drift across many iterations
No cross model failure pattern analysis
Text level mutation
Only reports failures without any insight on root-cause
Enforce norm-bounded perturbation with respect to original seed
Applies integer perturbations. Shows failure stability empricaclly. Shows ADAPT, DLFUZZ, DeepExplore failures are unstable
Uses differential testing based oracle to detect failure, not to check transferability from one to another.
Demostrates its ability to bypass adversarail defense mechanisms
aims to maximize model uncertainty and reveal generic misclassifications
Shows correlatin
The data generation loop (figure 2b) does not account cumulative accumulation of perturbation across iterations
Applies small floating point perturbations without clipping/rounding
Tests whether the same inputs still cause failures on the defended model (RQ4) but lacks explicit mechansim to discover transferable attacks
Expose  safety critical violations that lead to crashes/collisions or unsafe actions in autonomous or multi-agent environments.
Targets a class of faults (collission or dangerous states)
Provides correlation with internal coverage
After each mutation, it uses CARLA to validate and reject abnormal inputs
Perturbs continuous-valued initial state vectors within valid physical bounds and validates each mutated state through the simulator
No cross model failure pattern analysis
